summary: Iteration 11 — Whitelist disabled (ALLOWED_EMAILS=''). Backend-only smoke test. 12/12 pytest tests pass: unauth returns 401, public /api/ returns 200, and a NEW non-whitelisted user (test.iter11+*@example.com) can access /auth/me, /draws, /grids/verify, /grids/wheel, /stats/frequency. Regression: labouyrie28@gmail.com session still works. Auto-load code path (/auth/session -> _load_official_for_user on new users) confirmed present in server.py.
critical_code_review_comments: Resend is still in sandbox mode: /alerts/send will succeed only for labouyrie28@gmail.com. New users who opt-in to alerts will get 500 errors from Resend until sender domain is verified. Consider surfacing a friendly warning in the UI or catching the Resend rejection more gracefully.

Auto-load in /auth/session triggers a 1048-row CSV insert synchronously on first login — first-login latency may be noticeable (still under a couple of seconds locally, but consider running it in the background).
test_credentials: Ephemeral mongosh-injected sessions. Real user labouyrie28@gmail.com not modified.
seed_data_creation: One ephemeral non-whitelisted user (test.iter11+*@example.com) + one ephemeral session for labouyrie28@gmail.com, both cleaned up in fixture teardown. 30 seed draws inserted for the new user (cleaned up).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Whitelist is now OPEN (ALLOWED_EMAILS=''). Anyone with a valid Google session_token can use the app. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter11_open_access.py for regression. Note: Resend email sending is still restricted to labouyrie28@gmail.com by Resend sandbox mode — this is external, not a code bug.
rca of the issue: N/A — config change validation. server.py:97-99 gates on `if allowed and ...`; when ALLOWED_EMAILS='' the list is empty and the 403 branch is skipped. Verified end-to-end that a brand-new user reaches all protected endpoints with 200 and identifies as themselves via /auth/me.
